# synology-apm activity — Backup / Restore Activity History

Activities are split into **backup** and **restore**, each with `list`, `get`, and
`cancel`. By default `list` shows ongoing activities; pass `--history` for completed
ones.

See [apm-shared](../apm-shared/SKILL.md) for global options, output formats, and
confirmation rules.

## Commands

### `synology-apm activity backup list`

List backup activity records.

```
Usage: synology-apm activity backup list [OPTIONS]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--status <STATUS, ...>` | Repeatable: queuing / backing_up / canceling / success / failed / partial / canceled |
| `--search <SEARCH>` | Keyword search |
| `--machine-type <MACHINE_TYPE, ...>` | Machine sub-type filter, repeatable: pc / ps / vm / fs |
| `--m365-type <M365_TYPE, ...>` | M365 service type filter, repeatable: exchange / onedrive / chat / sharepoint / teams / group |
| `--namespace, -n <NAMESPACE, ...>` | Repeatable. Show only activities on the specified backup server(s) (get namespace from synology-apm infra server list --verbose) |
| `--since <SINCE>` | Start time: 30m / 1h / 24h / 7d or ISO 8601 (e.g. 2026-04-01T00:00:00) |
| `--until <UNTIL>` | End time: 30m / 1h / 24h / 7d or ISO 8601 (e.g. 2026-04-20T23:59:59) |
| `--history` | Show completed activities instead of ongoing activities |

### `synology-apm activity backup get`

Show backup activity details and logs.

**Search mode (by workload name, returns the latest):**

```sh
synology-apm activity backup get <WORKLOAD_NAME>
```

**Direct mode (by Activity ID):**

```sh
synology-apm activity backup get --id <activity-id>
```

```
Usage: synology-apm activity backup get [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | Workload name (search mode; returns the latest) |
| `--id <ACTIVITY_ID>` | Activity ID (direct mode) |

### `synology-apm activity backup cancel`

Cancel a running backup activity (requires confirmation).

**Example:**

```sh
synology-apm activity backup cancel --id <activity-id>
```

```
Usage: synology-apm activity backup cancel [OPTIONS]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--id <ACTIVITY_ID>` | Activity ID (from synology-apm activity backup list) |
| `--yes, -y` | Skip confirmation prompt |

> This action prompts for confirmation. Confirm with the user before running it, and pass `--yes` only once they have explicitly agreed.

### `synology-apm activity restore list`

List restore activity records.

```
Usage: synology-apm activity restore list [OPTIONS]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--status <STATUS, ...>` | Repeatable: preparing / restoring / canceling / ready_for_migrate / migrate_vm_manually / migrating / success / failed / partial / canceled |
| `--search <SEARCH>` | Keyword search |
| `--since <SINCE>` | Start time: 30m / 1h / 24h / 7d or ISO 8601 (e.g. 2026-04-01T00:00:00) |
| `--until <UNTIL>` | End time: 30m / 1h / 24h / 7d or ISO 8601 (e.g. 2026-04-20T23:59:59) |
| `--history` | Show completed activities instead of ongoing activities |

### `synology-apm activity restore get`

Show restore activity details and logs.

**Search mode (by workload name, returns the latest):**

```sh
synology-apm activity restore get <WORKLOAD_NAME>
```

**Direct mode (by Activity ID):**

```sh
synology-apm activity restore get --id <activity-id>
```

```
Usage: synology-apm activity restore get [OPTIONS] [NAME]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `[NAME]` | Workload name (search mode; returns the latest) |
| `--id <ACTIVITY_ID>` | Activity ID (direct mode) |

### `synology-apm activity restore cancel`

Cancel a running restore activity (requires confirmation).

**Example:**

```sh
synology-apm activity restore cancel --id <activity-id>
```

```
Usage: synology-apm activity restore cancel [OPTIONS]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--id <ACTIVITY_ID>` | Activity ID (from synology-apm activity restore list) |
| `--yes, -y` | Skip confirmation prompt |

> This action prompts for confirmation. Confirm with the user before running it, and pass `--yes` only once they have explicitly agreed.

## Tips

- `get` without `--id` searches by workload `NAME` and returns its latest activity.
- Filter `list` by `--status` (repeatable), `--search` (keyword), or a
  `--since`/`--until` time range.
- `activity backup list` additionally supports `--machine-type`/`--m365-type`
  (repeatable) and `--namespace` (repeatable, backup server namespace); these are not
  available for `activity restore list`.
- `cancel` requires `--id <activity-id>` (from `list`) and prompts for confirmation —
  confirm with the user, then pass `--yes` to proceed.
